name: Daniel Okafor
archetype: Long-term UC claimant returning to work
summary: Forklift driver, 9 months out of work after warehouse closed. Confident, ready to move, mild money worry. The default ON-TRACK Restart success story.
headline_moment: Lands Warehouse Team Member at Sundown Logistics, Stratford. UC tapers, +£3 200/yr take-home.

name: Amara Adekoya
summary: Trained admin worker, out 4 years caring for her elderly mother (now in residential care). Confidence dipped, CV gap explains itself but she needs help reframing it. Childcare cost for a 6-year-old is the live barrier.
headline_moment: After in-person re-engagement and a £30/wk childcare grant, lands a school-hours admin role at a local NHS GP surgery.

name: Wojciech Nowak
summary: Polish-speaking site supervisor, 28 years on UK construction sites, made redundant when the firm folded. Reads English well, prefers to speak Polish for nuance. Wants supervisor-level work, not entry. Wife works part-time.
headline_moment: Live Polish-English translator added to every Priya meeting. Lands Site Supervisor role at Brent Park Developments via Restart's CSCS Gold Card top-up.

name: Tomasz Iwanski
summary: Bright, articulate, last job was warehouse picker 11 months ago. Anxiety and sleep have got worse since being out of work. Lives alone. Has not been opening the app or replying to texts for 3 weeks.
headline_moment: Max Navigator AT-RISK score triggers same-day call. Warm-referred to Access to Work Mental Health Support. Plan reset to 3 small tasks per week.

name: Fadumo Hassan
archetype: Refugee · resettled · Restart with REP overlap
summary: Somali, granted refugee status 14 months ago. Was a primary school teacher in Mogadishu. UK qualifications partly recognised. Husband and 2 children. Lives in Cardiff Bay. Wants to teach again but needs UK QTS or an alternative path.
headline_moment: Free Somali-English translation at every adviser meeting. Lands a Teaching Assistant role at Riverside Primary, Cardiff, with a path to UK QTS via Adult Skills Fund.

name: Mark Wright
summary: Former HGV driver, 19 months out of work since release. Lives with brother. Lost licence after conviction but eligible to reapply. Frustrated, motivated, low patience for hoops. Has not done anything online for years.
headline_moment: Restart funds HGV licence reapplication + Class C medical. Ban-the-Box employer Bardon Aggregates offers a 12-month yard role with HGV when reinstated.
